title: 'Mad Max' on the ocean: The cocaine-loaded speedboats racing to Europe's shores
subhead: In the Atlantic, hundreds of powerful go-fast boats, custom-built to transport tons of cocaine, retrieve shipments from vessels traveling commercial routes between South America, Africa and Europe, according to an investigation by Le Monde in partnership with NDR, WDR, Süddeutsche Zeitung, NRC, IrpiMedia and The Washington Post.

title: French PM visits Morocco to warm ties between Paris and Rabat
In the United States, 'Trump Accounts' prove a hit, with $1,000 in savings allocated to every American newborn
French parliament adopts assisted dying law supported by Macron
subhead: President Emmanuel Macron had promised an assisted dying law when he was re-elected for a second term in 2022, in a change seen as one of the most important social reforms since France allowed same-sex marriage in 2012.

title: Australia to toughen modern slavery penalty after US threatens trade tariffs
subhead: Weeks after the United States threatened trade tariffs over the issue, Australia has announced that large companies that fail to prevent modern slavery in their supply chains, including forced labor and debt bondage, will be held criminally liable.

title: Christopher Nolan struggles to match Homer in 'The Odyssey'
subhead: In his search for an original aesthetic capable of capturing the epic masterpiece of antiquity, the filmmaker opts for grandeur, with a prestigious cast and vast resources, but fails to convince.

title: As her deputy questions promise to ban headscarves, Le Pen doubles down
subhead: Jordan Bardella has expressed doubts about one of the Rassemblement National's key proposals. But Marine Le Pen is considering putting it to a referendum, if she is elected.

US targets ICC in new offensive
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

title: Ukraine expands attacks in Sea of Azov, a crucial maritime corridor for Russia
subhead: After choking off Crimea, which Moscow has occupied since 2014, Ukrainian drones are waging a relentless naval campaign against Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet,' which has been accused of evading sanctions. This has brought the traffic of these vessels on the Sea of Azov to a near halt.

title: From Paris to Nice, Macron marks his final Bastille Day with a series of commemorations
subhead: For his final national holiday as president, Emmanuel Macron presided over an unprecedented military parade and paid solemn tribute to the victims of the Nice terror attack.
